---
name: Methodology proposal
about: Propose a change to how a skill reasons, structures output, or applies the CORE.md principles
labels: methodology
---

## Which skill

<!-- Which skill are you proposing to change? -->

## Which CORE.md principle motivates this

<!-- Pick the one most central to your proposal:
- Audit Before Acting
- Context Before Compression
- Edge & Variance Scan
- Care Check
- Plural Interpretations

Then explain how the current skill falls short on that principle. -->

## What you propose to change

<!-- Describe the specific change: a step in the workflow, an output field, a quality gate, a constraint that's missing or wrong. Be concrete. -->

## What is lost by this change

<!-- Required. Every change has a cost — something that gets harder, compressed, or deprioritized.
What is it here? What edge cases are affected? What context becomes harder to preserve?
If you can't name a cost, you may not have thought about it enough yet. -->

## Alternative interpretations of this problem

<!-- Name at least two other ways to read the problem you're solving.
Your proposal is one reading. What are the others?
This is required — not to create obstacles, but because plurality is the method. -->

## How this affects downstream skills

<!-- If this changes a skill's output schema or structure, what downstream skills are affected?
Check PIPELINE.md for the handoff chain. -->

## Anything else

<!-- References, related issues, prior art in research methodology literature, etc. -->
